title: Yeast and Fermentation
content:
YEAST

Types: Compressed (cake) and Active Dry. Both produce carbon dioxide to raise dough.

Compressed Yeast: Living organism requiring 78-82°F for activation. Begins to die at 120°F. Keep refrigerated; good for 2 weeks fresh, 2 months frozen. One ⅗-ounce cake raises 4 cups flour in 1½-2 hours.

Active Dry Yeast: Granular form in airtight packages. Requires 105-115°F water to dissolve. Substitute: 1 package (1 scant tablespoon) dry = 1 cake compressed yeast.

Nutritional Yeast: De-bittered dry yeast without leavening power. Add 1-3 teaspoons per cup flour for nutrition.

FERMENTATION
Dissolve yeast in warm liquid (with small amount of sugar) 5-10 minutes. First rising: dough doubles in bulk (1-2 hours at 75-85°F). Punch down, allow second rise. Final rise in pans: dough should spring back slowly when pressed lightly.
